title: JWT Token Refresh Mechanism
description: JWT tokens have 7-day expiry without refresh. Users must re-login after expiry. Implement refresh tokens for seamless UX.
blocking_items: Mobile app can't handle grace period

correction_steps.details: Update backend/src/routes/auth.js login endpoint: 1) Generate short-lived access token (JWT, 15 min expiry). 2) Generate long-lived refresh token (stored in DB, 30 days expiry). 3) Return both tokens: { accessToken, refreshToken, expiresIn: 900 }
Create POST /api/v1/auth/refresh: 1) Accepts refreshToken in body. 2) Validates token against DB record. 3) Checks expiry. 4) Generates new accessToken. 5) Returns { accessToken, expiresIn: 900 }

Modify backend/src/middleware/auth.js: 1) If token expired, return 401 with code 'TOKEN_EXPIRED'. 2) Include message: 'Please refresh token'. 3) Don't return 'AUTH_INVALID_TOKEN' for expired tokens (allows client to distinguish)

Update lib/services/api_service.dart: 1) Catch 401 TOKEN_EXPIRED response. 2) Automatically call POST /api/v1/auth/refresh with stored refreshToken. 3) Update stored accessToken with new token. 4) Retry original request with new token. 5) If refresh fails, redirect to login
Use flutter_secure_storage package: 1) Store refreshToken in device keychain/keystore (encrypted). 2) accessToken in memory (cleared on app close). 3) Never log tokens. 4) Clear on logout
Optional: each refresh generates new refreshToken (rotation). 1) Invalidate old refresh token after issuing new. 2) Prevents token replay attacks. 3) DB tracks issued tokens, revokes old ones
Create POST /api/v1/auth/logout: 1) Invalidates refreshToken in DB (marks revoked). 2) Clears user session. 3) Deletes tokens from Flutter secure storage on client. 4) Returns success

Create test scenario: 1) Login, get tokens. 2) Wait for accessToken to expire (manually set short expiry in test). 3) Make API call, trigger 401. 4) Automatic refresh triggered. 5) Verify original request succeeds with new token

validation: Refresh tokens generated and stored securely; automatic refresh works transparently; expired tokens handled gracefully; refresh endpoint available and working
error_handling: If refresh fails: check refreshToken expiry (>30 days = revoked), check DB connection. If rotation fails: log error, skip rotation but don't fail refresh. Implement grace period (10 min) before forcing re-login
deliverables: Updated login endpoint with refresh token generation
Modified auth middleware with TOKEN_EXPIRED detection

title: Log File Management and Archival
description: Request logging creates large log files. Need archival strategy and disk space management for production.
blocking_items: Difficult to find logs from specific dates

correction_steps.details: Backend already has Winston log rotation (5MB per file, 5 file retention). Verify in backend/src/utils/logger.js: maxsize: 5242880 (5MB), maxFiles: 5. In production, increase retention: maxFiles: 30 (30 days if daily rotation)
Update Winston config: add daily rotation filename format using date package: 'logs/combined-%DATE%.log'. Creates separate log file per day, automatically rotates at midnight

Create backend/scripts/archive_logs.js: 1) Find logs >30 days old. 2) Compress with gzip (.log.gz). 3) Upload to S3 or backup storage. 4) Delete local copy after confirming upload. 5) Schedule as cron job (daily at 2am)
Update .env: LOG_LEVEL=info (dev, tracks all requests), LOG_LEVEL=warn (production, only warnings/errors). Test: in production, avoid verbose request logging; keep error logging

Set up log monitoring: 1) Alert if error rate >1% in 5 min window. 2) Alert if disk usage on /logs partition >80%. 3) Alert on specific error patterns (DB connection failures, auth errors). Use ELK stack, Datadog, or app-specific monitoring
Document: 1) Who can access logs (ops team). 2) How long retained (30 days hot, 1 year archived). 3) Where archived (S3 with encryption). 4) Audit trail for log access requests
Add request ID to each request: 1) Generate UUID on API entry. 2) Include in all log lines for that request. 3) Client includes in error reports: 'Request ID: xxx'. 4) Ops team uses to correlate logs
Create LOG_MANAGEMENT.md: how to view logs, how to search for errors, how to request archived logs, retention policy, disk space expectations

validation: Log rotation working (verified daily files); archival automatic (>30 days compressed); monitoring alerts functional; log access policy documented
error_handling: If disk fills: emergency script deletes oldest unarchived logs after alerting. If archive fails: logs retained locally with retry. If monitoring fails: silent, but alert issue filed
deliverables: Request ID tracking in all logs

title: XSS Input Escaping Verification
description: Verify that XSS escaping doesn't double-escape. Need to ensure backend escapes once, frontend renders safely without re-escaping.
blocking_items: Risk of double-escaped content in UI
Text appears with escape characters visible

correction_steps.details: Create SECURITY_ESCAPING.md: 1) Backend escapes input on storage (validator.escape()). 2) Content retrieved already escaped. 3) Frontend renders as-is (no additional escaping). 4) Only escape if frontend-generated user input
Test backend escaping: POST /api/v1/translations with signText: '<script>alert(1)</script>'. Verify response has escaped value: '<script>alert(1)</script>'. No double-escaping
In Flutter, render escaped text directly using Text widget: Text(model.signText) or similar. Flutter automatically handles HTML entities for text display. DO NOT use html package to unescape
Add test cases for common XSS patterns: '', '
Update backend/src/middleware/security.js: add CSP headers (via Helmet): no unsafe-inline, no unsafe-eval. Restricts where scripts can run even if injected
If using WebView in Flutter: 1) Disable JavaScript by default. 2) Whitelist trusted domains. 3) Never load user input as HTML/JS. 4) Use flutter_inappwebview with security options
Use automated tools: OWASP ZAP (scan backend), Flutter security scanner (analyze dependencies). Verify no XSS, SQL injection, CSRF vulnerabilities

validation: No double-escaped content in UI; XSS test cases pass (injected scripts not executed); CSP headers present; security scan clean
error_handling: If double-escaping detected: identify source (backend or frontend), remove redundant escaping. If new vulnerabilities found: prioritize patch, notify users if exposed
dependencies: OWASP ZAP or similar security tool

title: Translation Caching Strategy
description: No caching mechanism. Implement local caching in Flutter and optional backend caching for performance.
blocking_items: Repeated API calls for same translations

correction_steps.details: Use hive or shared_preferences package: 1) Cache translated values locally with key: {userId}_{signLanguageClass}. 2) On app startup, load cache. 3) Display cached results immediately. 4) Silently refresh from backend in background

Implement cache TTL: 1) Cache entries valid for 24 hours. 2) After 24 hours, mark stale but accessible (offline mode). 3) Refresh from backend on next online connection. 4) Manual refresh button: 'Sync latest translations'

Create lib/services/sync_service.dart: 1) On app launch, check for updates from backend. 2) Compare local cache version with server version. 3) Download new/updated translations incrementally. 4) Store sync timestamp for next sync
Optional: implement Redis caching on backend: 1) Cache frequent translation queries (>5 requests/hour). 2) TTL: 1 hour. 3) Invalidate on POST /translations. 4) Monitor hit rate

Test scenario: 1) Load translations online (cache populated). 2) Go offline (disable network). 3) Open app, verify cached translations available. 4) Attempt POST (queued for sync). 5) Go online, verify queued items synced
Add UI indicators: 1) Loading spinner while syncing. 2) 'Offline mode' badge if offline. 3) 'Last synced: X minutes ago' timestamp. 4) Manual sync button. 5) Error message if sync fails

validation: Cached translations display instantly; offline mode functional; cache invalidates correctly; background sync works; UI shows sync status
error_handling: If cache corrupted: clear and rebuild from backend. If sync fails: queue for retry, notify user. If storage full: LRU eviction of oldest entries

title: Health Check and Monitoring Enhancement
description: Health check endpoint limited in scope. Need comprehensive monitoring for production readiness.

correction_steps.details: Expand GET /api/v1/health response: 1) status (healthy, degraded, unhealthy). 2) timestamp (ISO-8601). 3) uptime (seconds). 4) database: { connected, latency_ms, collections_count }. 5) memory: { used_mb, total_mb, heap_percentage }. 6) version, environment, node_version

Create GET /api/v1/health/full (requires admin): 1) Database queries: find user (latency), insert test doc (latency, rollback). 2) External services: ML model availability (if applicable). 3) File system: logs directory writable. 4) Cache: Redis connectivity (if used). 5) Response time <1 second

Use monitoring tool (Datadog, New Relic, Prometheus): 1) Alert if health endpoint fails (5xx). 2) Alert if response time >1 second. 3) Alert if database latency >100ms. 4) Alert if memory usage >80%. 5) Alert if error rate >1% (errors per minute)

Use external monitoring service (UptimeRobot, Pingdom, StatusPage): 1) Ping health endpoint every 1 minute. 2) Alert if endpoint unreachable (3 consecutive failures). 3) Track uptime percentage. 4) Public status page showing API health
Implement Prometheus metrics: 1) track request count by endpoint. 2) Track response time distribution. 3) Track database query count/latency. 4) Track error rate. 5) Expose at GET /metrics

Set up monitoring dashboard (Grafana with Prometheus): 1) Graph: request rates, latencies, errors. 2) Graph: database performance. 3) Graph: memory, CPU. 4) Alerts summary. 5) Update frequency: real-time
Create MONITORING.md: 1) How to access health endpoints. 2) Expected response format. 3) Alert thresholds and escalation. 4) Dashboard URLs. 5) Incident response procedures

validation: Health endpoint returns comprehensive data; monitoring alerts functional; uptime tracked and alert on failures; dashboards showing real-time metrics
error_handling: If health check slow: optimize database queries, profile middleware. If alerts noisy: adjust thresholds. If monitoring tool down: manual health checks
